---
title: "LCSM-091: Do letter-sound and vocabulary gains predict later reading gains? (lagged change-on-change)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LCSM-091 is the **change-on-change** extension of the latent change-score model
(#229 specification 2, built to the signed-off spec
`notes/202607131530-lrp229-lcsm-change-change-spec.md`). LRP67 (LCSM-067) asks
the prior-*level* question: does where a child *stands* on letter-sounds (`L`)
and expressive vocabulary (`E`) predict how much their word reading (`W`) grows
over the next window? This model adds the literal reading of #229: does how much
a child's letter-sounds or vocabulary just *grew* predict how much their reading
grows next, over and above their levels?

## Model structure

The LRP67 McArdle latent change-score scaffold is kept: a latent logit
true-score $x_m[i,t]$ per measure $m \in \{W, L, E\}$, child $i$ and wave $t$,
with process noise and a masked Beta-Binomial observation model, so measurement
floors (the heavy word-reading floor at t1) and noise stay out of the change
estimates. The reading-change equation over transitions $w = 1..3$ is:

$$
\begin{aligned}
\overline{\Delta}_W[i,w] = a_W[\text{arm}, w] &+ b_W\,x_W + g_L\,x_L + g_E\,x_E \\
&+ h_L\,\Delta_L[w-1] + h_E\,\Delta_E[w-1] \quad (w \ge 2) \\
&+ d^{\text{age}}\,\text{age}
\end{aligned}
$$

with every prior-wave level $x[w-1]$, and $\Delta_c[w-1] = x_c[w-1] - x_c[w-2]$
the previous transition's **latent** change in $c$. $h_L$ and $h_E$ are the
headline coefficients — prior **change** → subsequent **change** — alongside
LRP67's retained level couplings $g_L$ / $g_E$. The `L` and `E` equations stay
bare (self-feedback + age + arm × window intercepts).

Two design features follow from machinery that landed after the spec was merged
(#250, `notes/202607141030-time-lagged-model-designs.md`):

1. **Arm × window change intercepts.** The $h$ terms exist only on transitions
   2–3 (the first transition has no prior change) — both **post-crossover**,
   exactly where the verified d-separation result says the randomised arm
   confounds every pooled coupling. The window-1 cell contrast is reported
   below as the usual ITT-suite consistency check.
2. **No adjuster covariate block.** Unlike LCSM-081's reverse couplings, no
   fittable verified measured backdoor set exists for couplings *into* reading
   (the LCSM-082 derivation put it at ~13 nodes), so the model keeps LRP67's
   W/L/E shape for the side-by-side $g$-vs-$h$ comparison and stays in its
   exploratory-association class.

**Reading rule:** every coupling is an **adjusted association** — latent general
ability is unblockable, and between- vs within-child variance are not separable
at this sample size (the RI-CLPM is not estimable), so a large $h_E$ must not be
read as "vocabulary growth drives reading growth". With only **two usable
transitions** at $n \approx 54$ the pre-specified deliverable is **direction
agreement** with the Phase-0 randomised mediation (`lrp-rli-med-059`/`064`,
which moved reading through letter-sounds) — not a precise gain split. The
contemporaneous form ($\Delta W \sim \Delta L + \Delta E$, same window) is
deliberately not fitted: direction-ambiguous, occasion-correlated and
regression-to-the-mean-prone.

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_lcsm.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
